title: Spring In War Time
content:
I feel the spring far off, far off,
The faint, far scent of bud and leaf,
Oh, how can spring take heart to come
To a world in grief,
Deep grief?

The sun turns north, the days grow long,
Later the evening star grows bright,
How can the daylight linger on
For men to fight,
Still fight?

The grass is waking in the ground,
Soon it will rise and blow in waves,
How can it have the heart to sway
Over the graves,
New graves?

Under the boughs where lovers walked
The apple-blooms will shed their breath,
But what of all the lovers now
Parted by Death,
Grey Death?
meaning:
Sara Teasdale's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Spring In War Time"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
